chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 774 - وَ رَوَى عَمَّارٌ اَلسَّابَاطِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي وَ عَلَيْهِ خَاتَمُ حَدِيدٍ قَالَ «لاَ وَ لاَ يَتَخَتَّمُ بِهِ لِأَنَّهُ مِنْ لِبَاسِ أَهْلِ اَلنَّارِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.774 - Ammar al-Sabati narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) regarding a man who prays while wearing an iron ring.
Imam (as) said: "No, and he should not wear it, for it is the attire of the people of the Fire."
